MINI MINUTES: Cases PTR-16-300133 and PTR-16-300391 have been consolidated and were heard together.
Appearances: Daniel Bernhard for petitioner Sue Fisher King; Edward Poole, for and with petitioner Elaine Anderson.

Mr. Bernhard informed the Court that counsel had met and conferred and were working out arrangements for a distribution to be made. Mr. Poole stated that he was in agreement that a distribution should be made soon, and that the parties were requesting a 30-day continuance of the hearing on the consolidated actions. The matter was continued to April 10, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

Petition For Order Concerning Internal Affairs Of A Trust, An Order Determining Proration Or, Alternatively, For Instruction
Off Calendar Dec-21-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Poole for and with (petitioner) Elaine Anderson. Conrad Donner for James West, Pamela Weber & Wade Bentson.

The Court stated that this is an amended petition for an order regarding how to handle the ambiguity and tax liability for beneficiaries and confirmed that there have been no objections. Mr. Donner stated that there have been no objections and no extrinsic evidence presented. Mr. Poole is in agreement. The Court stated that the ambiguity is by a choice the trustor made regarding certain individuals and put into a provision which would free them of tax liability. Mr. Poole that there was a close and personal relationship by these 4 beneficiaries with the decedent and that it is believed that by placing them in the paragraph with the charities it indicated that these gifts of stock should not be subject to taxes. The Court stated that the structure of the trust supports this and granted the petition for order concerning interal affairs of a trust, etc., (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
